Author: Adi Insights; Innovations
Title: I “Vibe Coded” Using Cursor (No Code Required)
Text:
Member-only story
I “Vibe Coded” Using Cursor (No Code Required)
Andrej Karpathy’s new philosophy changes everything. Here is how I built a RAG app by just talking to my computer.
Andrej Karpathy recently coined a term that broke my brain: “Vibe Coding.”
The idea is simple: You write code using Natural Language. You focus on the vibe — the product vision, the user flow, the aesthetics — and you let the LLM handle the tedious syntax, the debugging, and the library imports.
I decided to put this to the test.
I wanted to build a Private RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) App using Llama 3. But I didn’t want to write a single line of boilerplate.
I opened Cursor (my AI-first IDE), whispered my requirements, and watched the code generate itself. The result? A fully functional, privacy-focused Chat UI that runs locally.
Here is how I used “Vibe Coding” to build a production-ready app, and the exact code it generated for me.
The Setup: Your New Pair Programmer
If you aren’t using Cursor or Windsurf, you are coding in hard mode. These tools allow you to highlight code and press Cmd+K
(Mac) or Ctrl+K
(Windows) to edit it with natural language.
